narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which forecasts first-time bar exam success based on academic and background factors, estimated a 60.5% chance that you would not pass the bar on your first attempt—a threshold that leads to rejection to protect the school's outcomes. This prediction stemmed primarily from your Year 3 school decile ranking of 2, which stood out as the strongest factor pushing the model toward forecasting failure, since students who passed averaged around the 6th decile. Your Year 1 decile of 3 also contributed notably in the same direction, placing you below the typical 6th decile seen among successful bar takers. On a more positive note, your undergraduate GPA of 3.40 worked in your favor, as it exceeds the 3.24 average for those who passed, helping to pull the prediction somewhat toward success. While your high family income aligns with a smaller but successful subset of passers (only 8.5%), and your LSAT score of 35 is close to the 37 average, these played lesser roles compared to your decile rankings. Full-time enrollment, common among 93% of those who passed, didn't sway things much either. Overall, your profile compares to accepted applicants by having a stronger undergrad record but weaker early and later school performance, tipping the balance against you. The model focuses on patterns from past students, and in your case, those lower deciles signaled higher risk despite other strengths. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score closer to 37 or above, or strengthening your recent academic decile through additional coursework or a post-bac program. You might also gather letters highlighting your resilience and non-academic achievements, as schools value well-rounded applicants. Reapplying after addressing these gaps could shift your predicted odds significantly. Many students in similar positions have succeeded by targeting these areas—your solid UGPA gives you a great foundation to build on. Keep pushing; this is just one data point in your journey.
